text:
Proverbs 11:1 A false balance is an abomination to Yahweh,
 but accurate weights are his delight.
Proverbs 11:1 When pride comes, then comes shame,
 but with humility comes wisdom.
Proverbs 11:1 The integrity of the upright shall guide them,
 but the perverseness of the treacherous shall destroy them.
Proverbs 11:1 Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath,
 but righteousness delivers from death.
Proverbs 11:1 The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way,
 but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.

 but the unfaithful will be trapped by evil desires.
Proverbs 11:1 When a wicked man dies, hope perishes,
 and expectation of power comes to nothing.
Proverbs 11:1 A righteous person is delivered out of trouble,
 and the wicked takes his place.
Proverbs 11:1 With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor,
 but the righteous will be delivered through knowledge.
Proverbs 11:1 When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices.
 When the wicked perish, there is shouting.
Proverbs 11:1 By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted,
 but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
Proverbs 11:1 One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom,
 but a man of understanding holds his peace.
Proverbs 11:1 One who brings gossip betrays a confidence,
 but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.
Proverbs 11:1 Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls,
 but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.
Proverbs 11:1 He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it,
 but he who refuses pledges of collateral is secure.
Proverbs 11:1 A gracious woman obtains honor,
 but violent men obtain riches.
Proverbs 11:1 The merciful man does good to his own soul,
 but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.
Proverbs 11:1 Wicked people earn deceitful wages,
 but one who sows righteousness reaps a sure reward.
Proverbs 11:1 He who is truly righteous gets life.
 He who pursues evil gets death.
Proverbs 11:1 Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to Yahweh,

text:
Proverbs 21:1 The king’s heart is in Yahweh’s hand like the watercourses.
 He turns it wherever he desires.
Proverbs 21:1 Every way of a man is right in his own eyes,
 but Yahweh weighs the hearts.
Proverbs 21:1 To do righteousness and justice
 is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.
Proverbs 21:1 A high look and a proud heart,
 the lamp of the wicked, is sin.
Proverbs 21:1 The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit;
 and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.
Proverbs 21:1 Getting treasures by a lying tongue
 is a fleeting vapor for those who seek death.
Proverbs 21:1 The violence of the wicked will drive them away,
 because they refuse to do what is right.
Proverbs 21:1 The way of the guilty is devious,
 but the conduct of the innocent is upright.
Proverbs 21:1 It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop
 than to share a house with a contentious woman.
Proverbs 21:1 The soul of the wicked desires evil;
 his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.
Proverbs 21:1 When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom.
 When the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
Proverbs 21:1 The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked,
 and brings the wicked to ruin.
Proverbs 21:1 Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor,
 he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.
Proverbs 21:1 A gift in secret pacifies anger,
 and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.
Proverbs 21:1 It is joy to the righteous to do justice;
 but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
Proverbs 21:1 The man who wanders out of the way of understanding
